Pretty much everyone knows, it's just a page where you get opt-ins. So I'm like, Tony, you need to do this, and put the squeeze page, put this up there. Tony literally stops me and he's like, Jeff, what's a squeeze page? I was like, I'm sort of flabbergasted. I'm part of that culture. I assume everyone else has squeeze pages. Okay, Tony's running big companies, makes a lot of money, knows what he's doing, doesn't know squeeze pages. So I explained, gave him the 30 second version of a squeeze page. So that was one of the things I talked about in my videos, was you guys, you have the specialized knowledge that the rest of the world doesn't have. I also told the story of me going to a mastermind with some real business people, like not like my kind of business, but like making ball bearings and stuff like that, and how all those people, they just wanted my business. As soon as they heard about my business, they really wanted it. And I was like Dan said, there's always that grass, there's always greenery, but I know I didn't want the ball bearing business. I was pretty good with my business. But so I basically told the story of Tony and the story of these real business owners wanting what we have. The amazing business that we have that allows us to have this internet lifestyle. They want it, and they not only want it, but they're coming to get it. They're learning about us. They're coming to get it from us. They want this knowledge, and we have this first starter advantage on them. In the land of the blind, the one-eyed man is king. I hit that really hard because if someone's gonna buy this, they have to buy into the fact that they have the knowledge to be able to actually do it. Because when they're buying it, they're actually thinking about doing it. They're gonna, even if they're not gonna end up doing it, they think they're gonna do it, or at least they're telling themselves they're gonna do it. So if they're gonna tell themselves they're gonna do something, then they have to have the faith that they can actually do it. So a lot of this pre-launch was based in instilling this idea that they had this secret knowledge. Just by watching my video, they had enough knowledge about internet marketing that would blow away most people. So that was a big theme as we went through the pre-launch. We know something and they're after it. Marketing gods. This is something that I just sort of came up with on the fly when I was on the podium at that $25,000 event. And it turned out to be inspired. I mean, it was just like, at some point in there, I was trying to convince them how awesome they were because they were awesome. We put them through the selection process and they paid a fortune to be there. So I was trying to, you know, it was clear I had to instill in them that they were awesome if they were gonna go out and have success. And them going out and having success, that small select group, having some real success there was important to me to build my product down the road. And somehow down there, I just stopped them like, you are marketing gods. There are tons and tons of content creators, very few marketing gods. You're the marketing gods. And I'm sorry if I'm offending anyone here, but it just did come out spur of the moment. And they grabbed onto that and they started tweeting that and they put it on Facebook that they're officially marketing gods because I told them they were marketing gods. And this became a theme going forward that basically I could just instill this marketing godhood in these folks. And like, so down the road, when I go to buy the thing, who doesn't wanna become like, to spend five grand and become a god, right? If you can, and this is actually, I took that clip from the script. Most of these videos do tend to be scripted. And this is why I actually pulled it from the script. If you can speak internet marketing, you are in a separate class from the rest of the people walking the face of the earth. It worked. I don't, you guys are gonna have a hard time reading some of these slides. What I actually, I apologize for the world's ugliest. I'm not like a big design guy. And what I did was I cut some of the slides from my videos and I dropped them straight in here. So you're probably not gonna be able to read these things. But this is a slide from one of my pre-launched videos. And it just says the launch asset discovery process. A key tool in your arsenal in your client will think you walk on water. Again, hammering the idea that they're gonna have the authority once they go through this training. They won't know you're just following the product launch manager script. The initial timing discussion. Easy for you, but a big aha moment for your client. Planning the offer, your client will think they have this one nailed down, but you'll stump them in the first three minutes. You will see this pattern over and over. Even among your biggest and most prestigious clients, you'll be able to identify major gaps in their strategy and tactics simply by following your basic product launch manager training. So again, just instilling the idea that you can do it. That you have the secret sauce already built in you and now we're gonna just amp it up with this training. You, a knight in shining armor. I think that was the status one, number 15 here. Creating proof. I'm a big one on proof. And this actually, I did this launch in late 2009. So this is before the new FTC regulations. And one of the reasons I did it in late 2009 was because in early 2009, I was reading Dan Kennedy's newsletter and it said these changes are coming. And I'm like, okay, I'm gonna need some proof for this thing. So I, and I'm gonna need to create some proof and use the proof. So I better do it before the new regulations. So I surveyed my list. I love surveys. Key component of product launch for me. I surveyed my list.
